questions.questionText:
If the solution curve of the differential equation

$$(({\tan ^{ - 1}}y) - x)dy = (1 + {y^2})dx$$ passes through the point (1, 0), then the abscissa of the point on the curve whose ordinate is tan(1), is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the area of the region $$\left\{ {(x,y):{x^{{2 \over 3}}} + {y^{{2 \over 3}}} \le 1,\,x + y \ge 0,\,y \ge 0} \right\}$$ is A, then $${{256A} \over \pi }$$ is equal to __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$ be the roots of the equation $${x^2} - 4\lambda x + 5 = 0$$ and $$\alpha$$, $$\gamma$$ be the roots of the equation $${x^2} - \left( {3\sqrt 2 + 2\sqrt 3 } \right)x + 7 + 3\lambda \sqrt 3 = 0$$, $$\lambda$$ > 0. If $$\beta + \gamma = 3\sqrt 2 $$, then $${(\alpha + 2\beta + \gamma )^2}$$ is equal to __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If m and n respectively are the number of local maximum and local minimum points of the function $$f(x) = \int\limits_0^{{x^2}} {{{{t^2} - 5t + 4} \over {2 + {e^t}}}dt} $$, then the ordered pair (m, n) is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The shortest distance between the lines

$${{x - 3} \over 2} = {{y - 2} \over 3} = {{z - 1} \over { - 1}}$$ and $${{x + 3} \over 2} = {{y - 6} \over 1} = {{z - 5} \over 3}$$, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let f be a differentiable function in $$\left( {0,{\pi \over 2}} \right)$$. If $$\int\limits_{\cos x}^1 {{t^2}\,f(t)dt = {{\sin }^3}x + \cos x} $$, then $${1 \over {\sqrt 3 }}f'\left( {{1 \over {\sqrt 3 }}} \right)$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the equation of the parabola, whose vertex is at (5, 4) and the directrix is $$3x + y - 29 = 0$$, is $${x^2} + a{y^2} + bxy + cx + dy + k = 0$$, then $$a + b + c + d + k$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If a point A(x, y) lies in the region bounded by the y-axis, straight lines 2y + x = 6 and 5x $$-$$ 6y = 30, then the probability that y < 1 is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The number of points of intersection of

$$|z - (4 + 3i)| = 2$$ and $$|z| + |z - 4| = 6$$, z $$\in$$ C, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The set of values of k, for which the circle $$C:4{x^2} + 4{y^2} - 12x + 8y + k = 0$$ lies inside the fourth quadrant and the point $$\left( {1, - {1 \over 3}} \right)$$ lies on or inside the circle C, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The value of $$\cot \left( {\sum\limits_{n = 1}^{50} {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {{1 \over {1 + n + {n^2}}}} \right)} } \right)$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let S = {E1, E2, ........., E8} be a sample space of a random experiment such that $$P({E_n}) = {n \over {36}}$$ for every n = 1, 2, ........, 8. Then the number of elements in the set $$\left\{ {A \subseteq S:P(A) \ge {4 \over 5}} \right\}$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\overrightarrow a $$ and $$\overrightarrow b $$ be the vectors along the diagonals of a parallelogram having area $$2\sqrt 2 $$. Let the angle between $$\overrightarrow a $$ and $$\overrightarrow b $$ be acute, $$|\overrightarrow a | = 1$$, and $$|\overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow b | = |\overrightarrow a \times \overrightarrow b |$$. If $$\overrightarrow c = 2\sqrt 2 \left( {\overrightarrow a \times \overrightarrow b } \right) - 2\overrightarrow b $$, then an angle between $$\overrightarrow b $$ and $$\overrightarrow c $$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If a1, a2, a3 ...... and b1, b2, b3 ....... are A.P., and a1 = 2, a10 = 3, a1b1 = 1 = a10b10, then a4 b4 is equal to -
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The mean and variance of the data 4, 5, 6, 6, 7, 8, x, y, where x < y, are 6 and $${9 \over 4}$$ respectively. Then $${x^4} + {y^2}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$y = y(x)$$ be the solution of the differential equation $$(1 - {x^2})dy = \left( {xy + ({x^3} + 2)\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right)dx, - 1 < x < 1$$, and $$y(0) = 0$$. If $$\int_{{{ - 1} \over 2}}^{{1 \over 2}} {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} y(x)dx = k} $$, then k$$-$$1 is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : A time varying electric field is a source of changing magnetic field and vice-versa. Thus a disturbance in electric or magnetic field creates EM waves.

Statement II : In a material medium, the EM wave travels with speed $$v = {1 \over {\sqrt {{\mu _0}{ \in _0}} }}$$. In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(C) Statement I is correct but Statement II is false
(D) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is true
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Two long parallel conductors S1 and S2 are separated by a distance 10 cm and carrying currents of 4A and 2A respectively. The conductors are placed along x-axis in X-Y plane. There is a point P located between the conductors (as shown in figure).

A charge particle of 3$$\pi$$ coulomb is passing through the point P with velocity $$\overrightarrow v = (2\widehat i + 3\widehat j)$$ m/s; where $$\widehat i$$ and $$\widehat j$$ represents unit vector along x & y axis respectively.

The force acting on the charge particle is $$4\pi \times {10^{ - 5}}( - x\widehat i + 2\widehat j)$$ N. The value of x is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : In hydrogen atom, the frequency of radiation emitted when an electron jumps from lower energy orbit (E1) to higher energy orbit (E2), is given as hf = E1 $$-$$ E2

Statement II : The jumping of electron from higher energy orbit (E2) to lower energy orbit (E1) is associated with frequency of radiation given as f = (E2 $$-$$ E1)/h

This condition is Bohr's frequency condition.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
(C) Statement I is correct but Statement II is false.
(D) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is true.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Three identical charged balls each of charge 2 C are suspended from a common point P by silk threads of 2 m each (as shown in figure). They form an equilateral triangle of side 1m.

The ratio of net force on a charged ball to the force between any two charged balls will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A lead bullet penetrates into a solid object and melts. Assuming that 40% of its kinetic energy is used to heat it, the initial speed of bullet is :

(Given : initial temperature of the bullet = 127$$^\circ$$C, Melting point of the bullet = 327$$^\circ$$C, Latent heat of fusion of lead = 2.5 $$\times$$ 104 J kg$$-$$1, Specific heat capacity of lead = 125 J/kg K)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If L, C and R are the self inductance, capacitance and resistance respectively, which of the following does not have the dimension of time?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
According to kinetic theory of gases,

A. The motion of the gas molecules freezes at 0$$^\circ$$C.

B. The mean free path of gas molecules decreases if the density of molecules is increased.

C. The mean free path of gas molecules increases if temperature is increased keeping pressure constant.

D. Average kinetic energy per molecule per degree of freedom is $${3 \over 2}{k_B}T$$ (for monoatomic gases).

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: When a ball is dropped into a lake from a height 4.9 m above the water level, it hits the water with a velocity v and then sinks to the bottom with the constant velocity v. It reaches the bottom of the lake 4.0 s after it is dropped. The approximate depth of the lake is :
questions.subject: Physics

(Given : $$\pi$$ = 3.14)

questions.questionText: A convex lens has power P. It is cut into two halves along its principal axis. Further one piece (out of the two halves) is cut into two halves perpendicular to the principal axis (as shown in figures). Choose the incorrect option for the reported pieces.

questions.questionText: If a wave gets refracted into a denser medium, then which of the following is true?
options: (B) wavelength increases, sped decreases and frequency remains constant.
(C) wavelength and speed decreases but frequency remains constant.

questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The distance of the Sun from earth is 1.5 $$\times$$ 1011 m and its angular diameter is (2000) s when observed from the earth. The diameter of the Sun will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: One end of a massless spring of spring constant k and natural length l0 is fixed while the other end is connected to a small object of mass m lying on a frictionless table. The spring remains horizontal on the table. If the object is made to rotate at an angular velocity $$\omega$$ about an axis passing through fixed end, then the elongation of the spring will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A stone tide to a spring of length L is whirled in a vertical circle with the other end of the spring at the centre. At a certain instant of time, the stone is at its lowest position and has a speed u. The magnitude of change in its velocity, as it reaches a position where the string is horizontal, is $$\sqrt {x({u^2} - gL)} $$. The value of x is -
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A parallel plate capacitor is made up of stair like structure with a plate area A of each stair and that is connected with a wire of length b, as shown in the figure. The capacitance of the arrangement is $${x \over {15}}{{{ \in _0}A} \over b}$$. The value of x is ____________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A mass of 10 kg is suspended vertically by a rope of length 5 m from the roof. A force of 30 N is applied at the middle point of rope in horizontal direction. The angle made by upper half of the rope with vertical is $$\theta$$ = tan$$-$$1 (x $$\times$$ 10$$-$$1). The value of x is ____________.

(Given, g = 10 m/s2)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle executes simple harmonic motion. Its amplitude is 8 cm and time period is 6 s. The time it will take to travel from its position of maximum displacement to the point corresponding to half of its amplitude, is ___________ s.

(B)
(C)
(D)

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Fluorine forms one oxoacid.

Reason R : Fluorine has smallest size amongst all halogens and is highly electronegative.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A.
(B) Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
(C) A is correct but R is not correct.
(D) A is not correct but R is correct.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A diatomic gas ($$\gamma$$ = 1.4) does 400J of work when it is expanded isobarically. The heat given to the gas in the process is __________ J.

Arrange the following coordination compounds in the increasing order of magnetic moments. (Atomic numbers : Mn = 25; Fe = 26)

A. [FeF6]3$$-$$

B. [Fe(CN)6]3$$-$$

C. [MnCl6]3$$-$$ (high spin)

D. [Mn(CN)6]3$$-$$

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements.

Statement I : Maltose has two $$\alpha$$-D-glucose units linked at C1 and C4 and is a reducing sugar.

Statement II : Maltose has two monosaccharides : $$\alpha$$-D-glucose and $$\beta$$-D-glucose linked at C1 and C6 and it is a non-reducing sugar.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
(D) Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

List-I
(Anion)		 List-II

(A) CO$${_3^{2 - }}$$ I. Colourless gas which turns lead acetate paper black.

(B) The geometry of PCl<sub>5</sub> is trigonal bipyramidal.
(C) PCl<sub>5</sub> has two axial bonds stronger than three equatorial bonds.
(D) The three equatorial bonds of PCl<sub>5</sub> lie in a plane.

(B) II. $$N{a_2}C{r_2}{O_7}/{H_2}S{O_4}$$

(D) IV. $$CHC{l_3}/NaOH$$

n 1 m$$_1$$
A. 3 3 $$ - $$3
B. 3 2 $$ - $$2
C. 2 1 +1
D. 2 2 +2

For the reaction taking place in the cell :

Pt (s)| H2 (g)|H+(aq) || Ag+(aq) |Ag (s)

E$$_{cell}^o$$ = + 0.5332 V.

The value of $$\Delta$$fG$$^\circ$$ is ______________ kJ mol$$-$$1. (in nearest integer)

questions.questionText:
0.25 g of an organic compound containing chlorine gave 0.40 g of silver chloride in Carius estimation. The percentage of chlorine present in the compound is ___________. [in nearest integer]

(Given : Molar mass of Ag is 108 g mol$$-$$1 and that of Cl is 35.5 g mol$$-$$1)

questions.questionText:
It has been found that for a chemical reaction with rise in temperature by 9 K the rate constant gets doubled. Assuming a reaction to be occurring at 300 K, the value of activation energy is found to be ____________ kJ mol$$-$$1. [nearest integer]

(Given ln10 = 2.3, R = 8.3 J K$$-$$1 mol$$-$$1, log 2 = 0.30)

questions.questionText:
Let [t] denote the greatest integer $$\le$$ t and {t} denote the fractional part of t. The integral value of $$\alpha$$ for which the left hand limit of the function

$$f(x) = [1 + x] + {{{\alpha ^{2[x] + {\{x\}}}} + [x] - 1} \over {2[x] + \{ x\} }}$$ at x = 0 is equal to $$\alpha - {4 \over 3}$$, is _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A solution containing 2.5 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$3 kg of a solute dissolved in 75 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$3 kg of water boils at 373.535 K. The molar mass of the solute is ____________ g mol$$-$$1. [nearest integer] (Given : Kb(H2O) = 0.52 K kg mol$$-$$1 and boiling point of water = 373.15 K)

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$f(x) = \left| {\matrix{ a & { - 1} & 0 \cr {ax} & a & { - 1} \cr {a{x^2}} & {ax} & a \cr } } \right|,\,a \in R$$. Then the sum of the squares of all the values of a, for which $$2f'(10) - f'(5) + 100 = 0$$, is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let A be a matrix of order 2 $$\times$$ 2, whose entries are from the set {0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5}. If the sum of all the entries of A is a prime number p, 2 < p < 8, then the number of such matrices A is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let for some real numbers $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$, $$a = \alpha - i\beta $$. If the system of equations $$4ix + (1 + i)y = 0$$ and $$8\left( {\cos {{2\pi } \over 3} + i\sin {{2\pi } \over 3}} \right)x + \overline a y = 0$$ has more than one solution, then $${\alpha \over \beta }$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the sum of the coefficients of all the positive powers of x, in the Binomial expansion of $${\left( {{x^n} + {2 \over {{x^5}}}} \right)^7}$$ is 939, then the sum of all the possible integral values of n is _________.

questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A metallic rod of length 20 cm is placed in North-South direction and is moved at a constant speed of 20 m/s towards East. The horizontal component of the Earth's magnetic field at that place is 4 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$3 T and the angle of dip is 45$$^\circ$$. The emf induced in the rod is ___________ mV.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
We do not transmit low frequency signal to long distances because-

(a) The size of the antenna should be comparable to signal wavelength which is unreal solution for a signal of longer wavelength.

(b) Effective power radiated by a long wavelength baseband signal would be high.

(c) We want to avoid mixing up signals transmitted by different transmitter simultaneously.

(d) Low frequency signal can be sent to long distances by superimposing with a high frequency wave as well.

Therefore, the most suitable option will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The cut-off voltage of the diodes (shown in figure) in forward bias is 0.6 V. The current through the resister of 40 $$\Omega$$ is __________ mA.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A rolling wheel of 12 kg is on an inclined plane at position P and connected to a mass of 3 kg through a string of fixed length and pulley as shown in figure. Consider PR as friction free surface. The velocity of centre of mass of the wheel when it reaches at the bottom Q of the inclined plane PQ will be $${1 \over 2}\sqrt {xgh} $$ m/s. The value of x is ___________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Let a circle C of radius 5 lie below the x-axis. The line L1 : 4x + 3y + 2 = 0 passes through the centre P of the circle C and intersects the line L2 = 3x $$-$$ 4y $$-$$ 11 = 0 at Q. The line L2 touches C at the point Q. Then the distance of P from the line 5x $$-$$ 12y + 51 = 0 is ______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: On the surface of polar stratospheric clouds, hydrolysis of chlorine nitrite gives A and B while its reaction with its reaction with HCl produces B and C. A, B and C are, respectively :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The current density in a cylindrical wire of radius r = 4.0 mm is 1.0 $$\times$$ 106 A/m2. The current through the outer portion of the wire between radial distances $${r \over 2}$$ and r is x$$\pi$$ A; where x is __________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Which is true about Buna-N?
options: (A) It is a linear polymer of 1, 3-butadiene.
(B) It is obtained by copolymerization of 1, 3-butadiene and styrene.
(C) It is obtained by copolymerization of 1, 3-butadiene and acrylonitrile.
(D) The suffix N in Buna-N stands for its natural occurrence.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.subject: Chemistry

If rd and rp are the radii of their circular paths respectively, then the ratio $${{{r_d}} \over {{r_p}}}$$ will be $$\sqrt{x}$$ : 1 where x is __________.
questions.subject: Physics

List-I List-II

(B) Analgesic II. Reduces stress
(C) Tranquilizer III. Reduces fever
(D) Antacid IV. Reduces acidity (stomach)

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

(B)
(C)
(D)

questions.questionText:
Statement I : Leaching of gold with cyanide ion in absence of air / O2 leads to cyano complex of Au(III).

Statement II : Zinc is oxidized during the displacement reaction carried out for gold extraction.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(C) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
(D) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: When 5 moles of He gas expand isothermally and reversibly at 300 K from 10 litre to 20 litre, the magnitude of the maximum work obtained is __________ J. [nearest integer] (Given : R = 8.3 J K$$-$$1 mol$$-$$1 and log 2 = 0.3010)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: pH value of 0.001 M NaOH solution is ____________.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
116 g of a substance upon dissociation reaction, yields 7.5 g of hydrogen, 60 g of oxygen and 48.5 g of carbon. Given that the atomic masses of H, O and C are 1, 16 and 12, respectively. The data agrees with how many formulae of the following?

A. CH3COOH, B. HCHO, C. CH3OOCH3, D. CH3CHO
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: BeO reacts with HF in presence of ammonia to give [A] which on thermal decomposition produces [B] and ammonium fluoride. Oxidation state of Be in [A] is ____________.
